name: Stuffed Squash
recipeIngredient: 1 cup dried tomatoes (I use the dry ones, not packed in oil)
1 cup bread crumbs (or whole stale bread)
1 dash balsamic vinegar (or red wine vinegar)
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat the oven to 350°F.
2. Halve the squash and scoop out the insides. Place cut-side down in a baking pan.
3. Bake for about 20 minutes, or until it begins to get soft.
4. Soak the dried tomatoes and mushrooms separately in hot water.
5. Add the garlic to a food processor and process until completely chopped.
6. Add the stale bread to the food processor and pulse until large crumbs form.
7. Empty the contents into a medium mixing bowl.
8. Add the fresh mushrooms, artichoke, herbs, and salt to the bowl.
9. When the dried tomatoes and mushrooms have softened, chop them and add to the bowl.
10. Turn the squash halves over and fill with the mixture. Add water to prevent the squash from becoming too dry.
11. Bake for an additional 10 minutes. Serve hot.
